steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what causes meanders to form. The notes state: "A meander forms when water erodes sediment from one side of the riverbank and redeposits that sediment on the opposite side." The correct answer must identify this mechanism.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The erosion and redepositing of sediment from one side of a riverbank to the other forms meanders, or curves, over time.": identifies the cause — erosion and redeposition of sediment from one bank to the other — which is exactly what the notes say creates meanders. ✓
  • "High sinuosity is caused by the formation of many meanders, or curves, in a riverbank over time.": discusses sinuosity without explaining what causes meanders to form. ✗
  • "The meanders in the South Esk River will change shape and shift downstream over time.": describes meander behavior, not formation. ✗
  • "Over time, many meanders have formed in the South Esk River, a river in the United Kingdom with high sinuosity.": addresses meander existence but not the mechanism that creates them. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
